query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in California, within PG&E's territory, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
I have sufficient data from the OpenEI IURDB. The query for PG&E (EIA ID 14328) commercial tariffs returned the **E-19 "Medium General Demand TOU"** schedule, which is PG&E's rate class for customers with maximum demand of **500 kW to 999 kW** — exactly the >500 kW commercial segment requested. The schedule is split into voltage-level service classes (Secondary, Primary, Transmission), each functioning as a distinct service class with its own demand charges.

## Demand Charges for PG&E Commercial Customers >500 kW (Schedule E-19)

Source: OpenEI U.S. Utility Rate Database (IURDB) via `get_utility_tariffs`, Pacific Gas & Electric Co (EIA ID 14328), Commercial sector, effective Mar 2025. PG&E's E-19 schedule applies to customers whose maximum demand is **500–999 kW**.

PG&E demand charges come in two parts: a **flat (maximum) demand charge** billed on the monthly peak kW regardless of time, plus **time-of-use (TOU) demand charges** billed on peak/part-peak kW during summer periods.

| Service Class (E-19, by voltage) | Flat / Max Demand Charge | TOU Part-Peak Demand | TOU Peak Demand |
|---|---|---|---|
| **E-19 Secondary** | **$42.10 /kW/mo** | $16.44 /kW | $21.59 /kW |
| **E-19 Primary** | **$33.10 /kW/mo** | $14.86 /kW | $18.71 /kW |
| **E-19 Transmission** | **$16.47 /kW/mo** | $0 | $0 |

(Option R variants of the same classes carry identical demand charges; the off-peak TOU demand tiers are $0.)

### Direct answer — at least two classes with their demand charges:
1. **E-19 Medium General Demand TOU – Secondary voltage:** maximum-demand charge of **$42.10/kW per month**, plus summer TOU demand charges of **$16.44/kW (part-peak)** and **$21.59/kW (peak)**.
2. **E-19 Medium General Demand TOU – Primary voltage:** maximum-demand charge of **$33.10/kW per month**, plus summer TOU demand charges of **$14.86/kW (part-peak)** and **$18.71/kW (peak)**.

(A third: **E-19 Transmission voltage** — **$16.47/kW per month** flat demand, no TOU demand adder.)

**Notes / assumptions:**
- I queried PG&E by its EIA utility ID (14328) because using a San Francisco street address resolved to the municipal utility (City & County of San Francisco / CCSF), not PG&E.
- The OpenEI dataset's currently active PG&E commercial demand records returned were all variants of Schedule E-19 (the 500–999 kW class). PG&E also has Schedule **E-20** for customers ≥1,000 kW, but no active E-20 record was returned in this OpenEI pull, so the figures above are limited to what the IURDB data actually contained.
- The lower voltage levels (Secondary) carry the highest demand charges because the customer relies more on PG&E's distribution system; Transmission-level customers, who take power at high voltage, pay the lowest demand charges.
- All figures are $/kW; flat demand charges are monthly. Source: OpenEI IURDB, PG&E EIA ID 14328, accessed via the `get_utility_tariffs` tool.

steps.reasoning:
The API results are limited to E-19 records (the 500-999 kW class), and I'm only getting about 8 active commercial demand records repeatedly, which suggests these might be the only ones available in OpenEI's PG&E dataset. I have enough data to work with now.

E-19 is PG&E's "Medium General Demand-Metered TOU Service" for 500-999 kW customers, and the different voltage levels (Secondary, Primary, Transmission) each have distinct demand charge structures—the flat maximum demand charges range from $33.10 to $42.10/kW/month, with varying TOU demand tiers for part-peak and peak periods. This gives me multiple service classes with different demand charges to present, satisfying the requirement for at least two classes.
